step_by_step_instructions: Brown the ground beef in a large skillet. Drain excess fat and transfer the beef to a slow cooker.

Add the baked beans, pineapple bits, onion, green pepper, barbecue sauce, soy sauce, minced garlic, salt, and pepper to the slow cooker. Mix well, cover, and cook on low for 4 to 8 hours until bubbly.
To make the pineapple rings, beat the egg well. Mix in the all-purpose flour to form a thick batter. Dip the drained pineapple rings in the batter.
Heat the vegetable oil in a frying pan to medium hot. Fry the battered pineapple rings until golden, then drain on a rack or paper towels.
To serve, place the baked beans in a bowl and top with the fried pineapple rings.
recipe_title: Pineapple Baked Beans and Beef with Fried Pineapple Rings
original_instructions:
In a large skillet, brown the beef. Drain excess fat and transfer to a 5-quart slow cooker. Add the remaining ingredients, mix well, cover, and cook on low for 4 to 8 hours until bubbly.
To make pineapple rings, beat egg well. Mix in the flour and make a thick paste. Dip pineapple slices in the batter and fry in medium hot oil.
To serve, place baked beans in bowl and top with pineapple rings.
This recipe was provided by professional chefs and has been scaled down from a bulk recipe provided by a restaurant. The FN chefs have not tested this recipe, in the proportions indicated, and therefore, we cannot make any representation as to the results.

step_by_step_instructions: Heat the oven to 375 degrees F.
In a large bowl, beat the peanut butter and brown sugar with an electric mixer until creamy.
Beat in the egg, water, and almond extract until blended.
Add the flour, baking soda, and salt. Beat until evenly moistened.
Stir in the chocolate chips.
Drop the dough by tablespoonfuls onto a cookie sheet.
Flatten slightly in a crisscross pattern with a fork.
Bake for 8 to 10 minutes, or until golden brown.
Cool for 1 minute on the cookie sheet, then move to a rack to cool completely.
original_instructions:
HEAT oven to 375 degrees F.
BEAT peanut butter and brown sugar in large bowl with electric mixer until creamy. Beat in egg, water and almond extract until blended. Add flour, baking soda and salt. Beat until evenly moistened. Stir in chocolate chips. Drop by tablespoonfuls on cookie sheet. Flatten slightly in criss cross pattern with fork.
BAKE 8 to 10 minutes or until golden brown. Cool 1 minute on cookie sheet before moving to rack to cool completely.
ingredients: 1 1/4 cups Jif Omega-3 Creamy Peanut Butter
3/4 cup Pillsbury BEST All Purpose Flour

step_by_step_instructions: Peel and slice or cube the sweet potatoes. Cover them with water and bring to a boil.
Salt the water and cook the potatoes for 12-15 minutes, until tender.
Drain the potatoes.
Return the pot to the stove and warm the chicken stock over medium heat.
Whisk in the butter and orange marmalade until combined.
Add the cooked potatoes and mash the sweet potatoes.
Season with salt and black pepper and add a few dashes of hot sauce.
Serve immediately.
original_instructions: Cover potatoes with water and bring to a boil. Salt water and cook potatoes 12-15 minutes until tender. Drain the potatoes. Return pot to the stove and warm broth over medium heat. Whisk in butter and marmalade. Add potatoes and mash the sweet potatoes. Season with salt and pepper and a few dashes hot sauce. Serve immediately.
ingredients: 2 pounds, 2 to 3 large, sweet potatoes, peeled and sliced or cubed

step_by_step_instructions: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F.
Press out the pizza dough to about 1/2-inch thickness and cut into 3-inch rounds. Place the rounds on two baking sheets.
Brush the crusts with 1 tablespoon olive oil. Sprinkle with 1/2 cup mozzarella and 1/2 cup cheddar.
Top each pizza with the diced bell pepper, chopped red onion, and minced garlic, then add the diced grilled chicken and cooked bacon.
Sprinkle the remaining mozzarella and cheddar over the pizzas and drizzle with the remaining olive oil.
Bake until the crusts are lightly browned, about 25 minutes.
processed_ingredients: 1.0 chicken breast halves grilled and diced
0.25 lb bacon diced and cooked until crisp
original_instructions:
Watch how to make this recipe.
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F.
Press out the pizza dough to a 1/2-inch thickness and punch out rounds using a 3-inch biscuit cutter. Transfer to two baking sheets and brush the crusts with 2 tablespoons of the olive oil. Sprinkle with 1 cup of the mozzarella and 1 cup of the cheddar. Top with the bell pepper, red onion, garlic, chicken and bacon. Sprinkle the remaining 1 cup mozzarella and 1 cup cheddar over the pizzas and drizzle with the remaining 2 tablespoons olive oil. Bake until the crusts are lightly browned, about 25 minutes.
ingredients: Two 13.8-ounce cans premade pizza crust dough
3 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves, grilled and diced
1/2 pound bacon, diced and cooked until crisp

step_by_step_instructions: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a saucepan over medium heat. Cook the chopped onion until tender.
Stir in the rice and mix to coat with the oil.
Pour in the chicken stock and bring to a boil.
Cover and reduce heat to low. Cook for 20 minutes.
Remove from heat and let stand for 5 minutes. Serve hot.
original_instructions: Heat oil in a saucepan over medium heat. Cook onion until tender and stir in rice. Mix rice to coat and pour in chicken stock. Bring to a boil, cover and reduce heat to low. Cook for 20 minutes. Remove from heat and let stand 5 minutes. Serve hot.

step_by_step_instructions: Bring a kettle of salted water to a boil. Cook the sugar snap peas for 2 minutes.
Add the snow peas and green peas. Cook for 1 minute.
Drain the peas in a colander and rinse in cold water. Pat dry on paper towels.
In a small bowl, whisk together the rice vinegar, sesame oil, sesame seeds, light brown sugar, and soy sauce until the sugar is dissolved.
In a bowl, toss the pea shoots and peas with the dressing.
original_instructions:
In a kettle of boiling salted water cook sugar snap peas 2 minutes. Add snow and green peas and cook 1 minute. Drain peas in a colander and rinse in cold water. Pat dry on paper towels. In a small bowl whisk together vinegar, oil, sesame seeds, sugar, and soy sauce until sugar is dissolved. In a bowl toss pea shoots and peas with dressing.
In a kettle of boiling salted water cook sugar snap peas 2 minutes. Add snow and green peas and cook 1 minute. Drain peas in a colander and rinse in cold water. Pat dry on paper towels.
In a small bowl whisk together vinegar, oil, sesame seeds, sugar, and soy sauce until sugar is dissolved.
In a bowl toss pea shoots and peas with dressing.
ingredients: 1/2 cup fresh or thawed frozen green peas
1/2 to 1 tablespoon firmly packed light brown sugar
6 cups pea shoots, washed well and spun dry

step_by_step_instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F (or use a toaster oven if baking a single pizza).
Spread the sauce over the pita bread.
Top with the chopped smoked chicken (or leftover vegetables, drained beans, or drained canned tuna).
Sprinkle the shredded mozzarella (or ricotta or crumbled goat's cheese) over the top.
Place on a baking sheet and bake for 7 to 10 minutes, until the crust is crisp, the filling is hot, and the cheese has melted.
processed_ingredients: 1 white flour pita bread (about 6 inches across)
original_instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees or if you are are baking a single pizza use a toaster oven. Spread the sauce or other exotic jarred ingredients over the pita. Top with main ingredient and top with cheese. Set on baking sheet and bake for 7 to 10 minutes or until crust is crisp, filling is hot and cheese has melted.
ingredients: 1 white flour pita bread (about 6 inches across)
2 tablespoons spaghetti sauce, or pesto, or green olive puree or chopped sun-dried tomatoes or chopped roasted
salt and freshly ground black pepper
1/2 cup chopped smoked chicken or leftover vegetables or drained beans or drained canned tuna
1/2 cup shredded Mozzarella cheese or Ricotta or crumbled Goat's cheese

step_by_step_instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F.
Place the flour on a worktable and make a well in the center.
Add the sugar, extra-virgin olive oil, and white wine to the well.
Combine and knead until an even dough forms. If needed, add a little more flour to absorb all the liquids.
Sprinkle 1 tbsp of the anise seeds over the dough and knead well.
Sprinkle the remaining anise seeds over the dough and continue kneading until evenly distributed.
Cut off a small portion of dough and roll into a 1/2-inch thick log.
Cut the log into 5-inch long pieces.
Pinch opposite ends together to form a doughnut-like shape.
Repeat with the remaining dough.
Line a baking tray with parchment paper and arrange the ciambelle about 2 inches apart.
Bake for about 10 minutes, or until golden brown.
original_instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F. Place the flour on a worktable. Make a well in the center of the flour and add the sugar, extra-virgin olive oil and wine. Combine together and knead until the mixture becomes an even dough. If necessary, add a little more flour to absorb all the liquids. Sprinkle a tablespoon of anise seeds on the dough and knead well. Sprinkle the rest of the anise seeds, and continue to knead so that the seeds are evenly distributed. Cut off a small amount of dough and roll into a 1/2-inch thick log. Cut the log into a 5-inch long piece. Then form a 'doughnut-like' shape by pinching opposite ends together. Repeat the above process with the remaining dough. Line a baking tray with cooking parchment paper and arrange the ciambelle, approximately 2 inches apart. Bake for approximately 10 minutes or until golden brown.
ingredients: 4 to 5 cups/0.9 kg to 1.1 kg all-purpose flour

step_by_step_instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium heat. Add the butter and melt.
Add the sliced peaches and sprinkle with cinnamon. Cook for 3 minutes.
Turn the heat to medium-high. Add the brown sugar and stir until it melts and coats the peaches.
Remove the pan from the stove, carefully add the whiskey, then return the pan to the stove and tip the skillet to ignite the pan (or ignite with a kitchen match).
When the flame dies out, scoop ice cream into serving dishes, top with the peaches, and finish with whipped cream.
original_instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium heat. Add butter, melt and add peaches then season them with cinnamon. Turn up heat to medium-high and cook peaches 3 minutes then add sugar and melt into the butter and peaches. Pull pan off stove, add whiskey, return pan to stove and tip skillet to set pan aflame, or set aflame with a kitchen match. When the flame dies out, scoop some ice cream into dishes, top with peaches and whipped cream and serve.
ingredients: 4 tablespoons butter, cut into small pieces

step_by_step_instructions: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F.
For the zucchini salsa, puree the zucchini, onion, jalapeno, cilantro, and lime juice in a blender with 1 teaspoon salt and 1/4 teaspoon pepper.

For the quinoa filling, bring the quinoa, 2 cups water, and 1/2 teaspoon salt to a boil in a medium saucepan. Reduce to a simmer, cover, and cook until tender, about 10 minutes. Drain and toss with the olive oil, then let cool about 10 minutes.
Fold the goat cheese and raisins into the cooled quinoa.
Stuff the quinoa filling into the piquillo peppers and place them in a 2-quart baking dish. Cover with foil and bake until the filling is hot throughout, about 20 minutes.
Serve the baked peppers with the zucchini salsa.
original_instructions:
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F.
For the zucchini salsa: Puree the zucchini, onion, jalapeno, cilantro, lime juice, 1 teaspoon salt and 1/4 teaspoon pepper in a blender.
For the quinoa and goat cheese piquillo peppers: Bring the quinoa, 2 cups water and 1/2 teaspoon salt to a boil in a medium saucepan. Reduce to a simmer, cover and cook until tender, about 10 minutes. Drain and toss with the olive oil. Let cool about 10 minutes.
Fold in the goat cheese and raisins. Stuff the quinoa filling into the piquillo peppers and place them in a 2-quart baking dish and cover with foil. Heat the peppers until the filling is hot throughout, about 20 minutes.
Serve the peppers with the zucchini salsa.
ingredients: Kosher salt and finely ground black pepper
Kosher salt and finely ground black pepper1/2 cup golden raisins
Quinoa and Goat Cheese Piquillo Peppers:
One 14-ounce jar piquillo peppers, rinsed and drained
